# llms.txt — Comtrya Documentation Reference
# Source: https://comtrya.dev
# Last updated: 2026-04-14

> Comtrya is a permissively licensed Open Source tool built in Rust.
> It provisions and configures systems through simple YAML or TOML manifests.
> Source: https://github.com/comtrya/comtrya

## Supported Systems

- FreeBSD
- Linux
- macOS
- NetBSD
- Windows

## Installation

```bash
# Shell script (pre-compiled binary)
curl -fsSL https://get.comtrya.dev | sh

# Specific version
curl -fsSL https://get.comtrya.dev | VERSION=v0.9.2 sh

# Via cargo
cargo install comtrya
```

## CLI Usage

```bash
# Run all manifests in current directory
comtrya apply

# Run all manifests in a specified directory
comtrya -d ./manifests apply

# Run specific manifests
comtrya -d ./ apply -m one,two,three

# With a config file
comtrya -d ./ -c /path/to/Comtrya.yaml

# Remote manifests from a Git repository
comtrya -d https://github.com/user/repo apply

# Remote with branch and path
comtrya -d https://github.com/user/repo#main:dotfiles apply

# Define variables via CLI
comtrya -v -d ./ --defines foo=bar apply -m sample

# List available contexts (OS, user, env info)
comtrya contexts
comtrya contexts --show-values

# List manifest status
comtrya status

# Generate shell completions
comtrya gen-completions bash
comtrya gen-completions fish
```

## Manifests

Manifests are YAML or TOML files composed of actions and dependencies.
Each recipe directory has a `main.yaml` entrypoint, with optional `files/` subdirectory for assets.

### YAML Example

```yaml
actions:
  - action: command.run
    command: echo
    args:
      - hi
```

### TOML Example

```toml
[[actions]]
action = "command.run"
command = "echo"
args = [ "hi" ]
```

## Dependencies

Manifests can depend on other manifests, ensuring ordering:

```yaml
depends:
  - groups

actions:
  - action: user.add
    fullname: testuser
    home_dir: /home/test
    username: test
    group:
      - testgroup
```

## Contexts (Template Variables)

Comtrya provides context variables usable in manifests via Jinja-style templates `{{ ... }}`:

### Available Context Groups

- `env.*` — environment variables (COLORTERM, HOME, PATH, SHELL, etc.)
- `os.bitness` — e.g. "64-bit"
- `os.codename` — e.g. "noble"
- `os.distribution` — e.g. "Ubuntu", "Fedora", "Linux Mint"
- `os.edition`
- `os.family` — e.g. "unix"
- `os.hostname`
- `os.name` — e.g. "linux", "macos"
- `os.version` — e.g. "24.04", "43"
- `user.config_dir`
- `user.data_dir`
- `user.data_local_dir`
- `user.document_dir`
- `user.home_dir`
- `user.id`
- `user.name`
- `user.username`
- `variables.*` — user-defined variables from Comtrya.yaml or CLI --defines
- `include_variables.*` — included variable files

### Template Examples

```yaml
to: "{{ user.home_dir }}/.config/app/config.toml"
```

```yaml
# Tera filter for version parsing
list:
  - "https://example.com/release-{{ os.version | split(pat=\".\") | first }}.rpm"
```

Variables can be defined in `Comtrya.yaml`:

```yaml
privilege: sudo
variables:
  test: "one"
```

Or via CLI:

```bash
comtrya --defines foo=bar apply
```

Used in manifests as `{{ variables.foo }}`.

## Variants and Where Clauses

All actions support `where` for conditional execution and `variants` for per-condition overrides.

### Where Clause (skip/run actions conditionally)

```yaml
- action: command.run
  where: os.name == "linux"
  command: echo
  args:
    - Hello Linux
```

```yaml
- action: package.install
  where: 'os.distribution == "Ubuntu" || os.distribution == "Linux Mint"'
  name: curl
  provider: apt
```

### Variants (override action fields per condition)

```yaml
- action: command.run
  command: echo
  args:
    - hello, world!
  variants:
    - where: os.name == "linux"
      command: reboot
    - where: os.name == "macos"
      command: echo
      args: ["Hello", "macOS"]
    - where: os.distribution == "Ubuntu"
      command: echo
      args: ["Hi,", "Ubuntu"]
```

## Privilege Escalation

Actions can be run with elevated privileges:

```yaml
- action: command.run
  command: whoami
  privileged: true
```

Providers: `sudo` (default), `doas`, `run0`. Configured in `Comtrya.yaml`:

```yaml
privilege: doas
```

## Actions Reference

### command.run (alias: cmd.run)

Run arbitrary commands.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `command.run` |
| command | string | no | command to run |
| args | list[string] | yes | arguments |
| dir | string | yes | working directory |
| privileged | bool | yes | elevate privileges |
| env | map[string,string] | yes | scoped environment variables |

```yaml
- action: command.run
  dir: .
  command: echo
  args:
    - Hello world

- action: command.run
  command: go
  args:
    - env
  env:
    GOBIN: /Users/test
```

### file.copy

Copy a file. Source must be under a `files/` directory in the manifest.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `file.copy` |
| from | string | no | source file (relative to files/) |
| to | string | no | destination path |
| template | bool | yes | render with context providers (default: false) |
| chmod | integer | yes | octal permissions |
| owned_by_user | string | yes | user for chown |
| owned_by_group | string | yes | group for chown |

```yaml
- action: file.copy
  from: procs-config.toml
  to: "{{ user.config_dir }}/procs/config.toml"
```

### file.download

Download a file from a URL.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `file.download` |
| from / source | string | no | URL |
| to / target | string | no | local destination |
| owned_by_user | string | yes | user for chown |
| owned_by_group | string | yes | group for chown |

```yaml
- action: file.download
  from: https://example.com/file.tar.gz
  to: /tmp/file.tar.gz
```

### file.link

Create symlinks.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `file.link` |
| from | string | no | symlink location |
| to | string | no | symlink target |
| walk_dir | bool | yes | walk files in directory (default: false) |
| source | string | yes | used with walk_dir in lieu of from |

### file.remove

Remove a file.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `file.remove` |
| target | string | no | file to remove |

### file.chown

Change file ownership (requires root).

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `file.chown` |
| path | string | no | file path |
| user | string | no | owner user |
| group | string | no | owner group |

### file.unarchive

Extract a tar.gz archive.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `file.unarchive` |
| from | string | no | path to archive |
| to | string | no | extraction destination |
| force | bool | yes | force unarchiving (default: true) |

```yaml
- action: file.download
  from: https://github.com/example/repo/archive/refs/tags/v1.0.tar.gz
  to: /tmp/archive

- action: file.unarchive
  from: /tmp/archive
  to: /tmp/
```

### directory.copy

Copy a directory.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `directory.copy` |
| from | string | no | source directory |
| to | string | no | destination directory |

### directory.create

Create a directory.

```yaml
- action: directory.create
  path: "{{ user.home_dir }}/.config/app"
```

### package.install

Install packages via system package manager.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `package.install` |
| name | string | no | package name (or local file path) |
| list | list[string] | yes | multiple packages |
| provider | string | yes | package manager provider |
| repository | string | yes | specific repository |
| file | bool | yes | treat name as local file path (default: false) |

Supported providers: pacman/yay, paru (Arch), apt (Debian/Ubuntu), pkg (FreeBSD), pkgin (NetBSD), brew (macOS), winget (Windows), xbps (Void), zypper (OpenSUSE), dnf (Fedora), snapcraft (Linux), macports (macOS).

```yaml
# Default provider
- action: package.install
  name: curl

# Multiple packages
- action: package.install
  list:
    - curl
    - wget

# Specific provider
- action: package.install
  name: curl
  provider: apt

# Install from URL (supported by some providers like dnf)
- action: package.install
  list:
    - "https://example.com/package.rpm"

# Local file install (supported by pkg, aptitude)
- action: package.install
  name: /path/to/package.pkg
  file: true
```

### package.repository

Add a package repository.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| name | string | no | alias of url |
| key.url | string | no | key URL |
| key.name | string | yes | key name |
| key.fingerprint | string | yes | key fingerprint |
| provider | string | yes | package manager |

### binary.github

Download a binary from a GitHub release.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `binary.github` |
| name | string | no | local binary name |
| directory | string | no | target directory |
| repository | string | no | GitHub repo (owner/repo) |
| version | string | no | version/tag |

```yaml
- action: binary.github
  name: comtrya
  directory: /usr/local/bin
  repository: comtrya/comtrya
  version: v0.8.7
```

### git.clone (0.9.1+)

Clone a Git repository.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `git.clone` |
| repo_url | string | no | repository URL |
| directory | string | no | clone destination |

```yaml
- action: git.clone
  repo_url: https://github.com/comtrya/comtrya
  directory: /home/user/code/comtrya/
```

### group.add

Add a system group.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `group.add` |
| group_name | string | no | group name |

### user.add

Add a system user.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `user.add` |
| fullname | string | no | full name |
| home_dir | string | no | home directory |
| username | string | no | username |
| shell | string | yes | user shell |
| group | list[string] | yes | groups to add user to |

### user.group

Add an existing user to groups.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `user.group` |
| username | string | no | username |
| group | list[string] | no | groups to add to |

### macos.default

Set macOS defaults.

| Key | Type | Optional | Description |
|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| action | string | no | `macos.default` |
| domain | string | no | defaults domain |
| key | string | no | key name |
| kind | string | no | value type (string, bool, int) |
| value | string | no | value |
